items.headline: Sunnyvale to study eliminating chemical pesticides on city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will study a policy to ban chemical pesticide use on all city-owned and leased property. If adopted, this would affect parks, public buildings, and other municipal land.

items.headline: Sunnyvale to review artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will study the city's artificial turf policy. This could influence what materials are used in parks and recreational areas across Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Historic board considers demolition of Professorville building
items.summary: The Historic Resources Board is hearing a demolition application for a significant building at 439 Lincoln Avenue in the Professorville Historic District. The outcome will determine whether the structure can be torn down.

items.headline: Historic building could be relocated and converted to duplex
items.summary: The Historic Resources Board is reviewing a proposal to relocate an existing historic resource at 243 Webster Street and convert it into a duplex. The hearing will weigh whether the rehabilitation and conversion is appropriate.

items.headline: Setback requirement updated at 3300 El Camino Real
items.summary: City Council is amending the special setback requirement along a portion of Hansen Way at 3300 El Camino Real, changing it to a 20-foot setback. This affects how buildings must be positioned on the property.

items.headline: Planning Commission to hear rezoning and subdivision at 1341 Homestead Road
items.summary: The Planning Commission will hold a public hearing on removing historic designation from 1341 Homestead Road to allow subdivision of the property into two parcels, each housing one existing residence. 906 Monroe Street will retain its historic designation. CEQA review will assess consistency with the Downtown Precise Plan.

items.headline: Echo Place residential development receives permits for 6 new homes
items.summary: Building permits issued for a new residential development on Echo Place — five single-family homes and one duplex, seven units in total. The project adds housing stock in a coordinated neighborhood infill development.

items.headline: Library parcel tax rates increase for FY 2026-27
items.summary: San José City Council approved an increase to library parcel tax rates effective in FY 2026-27. Property owners will see higher assessments to fund library operations.
